Title: User Registration Success
Preconditions: MongoDB is running | Backend API is connected | Frontend is accessible
Steps: 1. Navigate to /register | 2. Fill in email, password (min 8 chars), full name, phone | 3. Click Register button | 4. Observe response
Test Data: Email: testuser@oktawave.com | Password: Test@1234 | Full Name: Test User | Phone: 555-0100
Expected Result: User account is created in MongoDB | JWT token is issued | User is redirected to /dashboard | Success message is displayed

Title: User Login Success
Preconditions: User account exists in database | Email: admin@oktawave.com | Password: Admin@123
Steps: 1. Navigate to /login | 2. Enter email and password | 3. Click Login button | 4. Verify redirect based on role
Test Data: Email: admin@oktawave.com | Password: Admin@123
Expected Result: JWT token is issued | User data is retrieved | Admin user redirects to /admin | Regular user redirects to /dashboard | Token stored in localStorage

Title: Vehicle Registration Success
Preconditions: User is authenticated | JWT token is valid | User is on /vehicles page
Steps: 1. Click "Register New Vehicle" button | 2. Fill license plate, make, model, color, year | 3. Submit form | 4. Verify vehicle appears in list
Test Data: License Plate: ABC123 | Make: Toyota | Model: Camry | Color: Blue | Year: 2023
Expected Result: Vehicle record created in MongoDB | Vehicle linked to user_id | Success message displayed | Vehicle appears in user's vehicle list | License plate is uppercase normalized

Title: Parking Reservation Success
Preconditions: User is authenticated | User has at least one registered vehicle | Available parking spots exist | User is on /reservations page
Steps: 1. View available parking spots grid | 2. Select an available spot by clicking | 3. Choose vehicle from dropdown | 4. Select start and end times | 5. Click Reserve Spot
Test Data: Spot: A01 | Vehicle: ABC123 Toyota Camry | Start: 2026-05-11 08:00 | End: 2026-05-11 17:00
Expected Result: Reservation created in MongoDB | Spot marked as unavailable (is_available = false) | Success message displayed | Spot moves to occupied list | Reservation includes user_id, spot_id, vehicle_id, timestamps

Title: Admin View Metrics
Preconditions: Admin user is authenticated | Admin role is verified | Admin navigates to /admin | Metrics tab is active
Steps: 1. Login as admin user | 2. Navigate to /admin dashboard | 3. View Metrics tab | 4. Verify all metric cards display correctly
Test Data: Admin credentials: admin@oktawave.com / Admin@123
Expected Result: Metrics display: total_users count | total_vehicles count | total_reservations count | active_reservations count | available_spots/total_spots ratio | Audit log entry created for 'view metrics' action

Title: Complete User Journey - Registration to Reservation
Preconditions: Clean database state | MongoDB is running | Frontend and backend are running | No prior user data
Steps: 1. Register new user account | 2. Login with new credentials | 3. Navigate to vehicle registration | 4. Register a vehicle | 5. Navigate to parking reservation | 6. Reserve a parking spot | 7. Verify reservation is created
Test Data: New user email: e2e@oktawave.com | Password: E2ETest@123 | Vehicle: XYZ789 Honda Accord | Spot: B05 | Time: Next day 9AM-5PM
Expected Result: User account created | JWT issued and stored | Vehicle registered and linked to user | Parking spot reserved | Spot availability updated | All data persisted in MongoDB | User can view their reservation

Title: Admin Management Workflow
Preconditions: Admin user exists | Regular users and reservations exist in database | Admin is authenticated
Steps: 1. Login as admin | 2. View system metrics | 3. Navigate to Users tab and view all users | 4. Navigate to Vehicles tab and view all vehicles | 5. Navigate to Reservations tab | 6. Cancel an active reservation | 7. Delete a user
Test Data: Admin: admin@oktawave.com / Admin@123 | Target user for deletion: testuser@oktawave.com | Target reservation: any active reservation
Expected Result: Metrics load correctly | All users displayed in table | All vehicles displayed | All reservations displayed with status | Cancelled reservation status updated to 'cancelled' | Spot freed (is_available = true) | User deleted from database | All admin actions logged in audit_logs collection

Title: User Registration with Duplicate Email
Preconditions: Existing user with email: duplicate@oktawave.com is in database
Steps: 1. Navigate to /register | 2. Attempt to register with existing email | 3. Submit form
Test Data: Email: duplicate@oktawave.com | Password: Test@1234 | Full Name: Duplicate Test
Expected Result: HTTP 400 Bad Request returned | Error message displayed: "Email already registered" | No new user record created | User remains on registration page

Title: Vehicle Registration with Duplicate License Plate
Preconditions: User is authenticated | Existing vehicle with license plate XYZ999 is already registered
Steps: 1. Navigate to /vehicles | 2. Click Register New Vehicle | 3. Enter license plate that already exists | 4. Submit form
Test Data: License Plate: XYZ999 | Make: Ford | Model: F-150 | Color: Red | Year: 2022
Expected Result: HTTP 400 Bad Request returned | Error message displayed: "License plate already registered" | No duplicate vehicle created | Form remains visible with error message

Title: Reservation Conflict - Overlapping Times
Preconditions: User is authenticated | User has a vehicle | Spot A10 has an active reservation from 10:00-14:00 on 2026-05-12
Steps: 1. Navigate to /reservations | 2. Select spot A10 (occupied) | 3. Attempt to reserve for overlapping time 12:00-16:00 | 4. Submit reservation
Test Data: Spot: A10 | Vehicle: User's vehicle | Start: 2026-05-12 12:00 | End: 2026-05-12 16:00
Expected Result: HTTP 409 Conflict returned | Error message: "Spot is already reserved for this time period" | No reservation created | Spot remains occupied | Original reservation unchanged

Title: Reservation Without Registered Vehicle
Preconditions: User is authenticated | User has NO vehicles registered | User navigates to /reservations
Steps: 1. View reservations page | 2. Attempt to select vehicle dropdown | 3. Attempt to submit reservation
Test Data: No vehicle data
Expected Result: Warning message displayed: "You need to register a vehicle first" | Link to /vehicles provided | Vehicle dropdown is disabled | Reserve button is disabled | User cannot submit reservation

Title: Admin Access by Regular User
Preconditions: Regular user is authenticated (non-admin role) | User attempts to navigate to /admin
Steps: 1. Login as regular user | 2. Manually navigate to /admin URL
Test Data: Regular user credentials (any non-admin user)
Expected Result: Access denied | User redirected to /dashboard | HTTP 403 Forbidden if API is called directly | Admin content is not rendered | useAuth isAdmin check prevents access

Title: JWT Token Validation and Expiry
Preconditions: User is authenticated | Valid JWT token exists in localStorage | Token has sub claim with user ID
Steps: 1. Make authenticated API call with valid token | 2. Verify token is validated on backend | 3. Verify user data is retrieved from MongoDB | 4. Verify protected route access is granted
Test Data: Valid JWT token with user_id in sub claim
Expected Result: Token is decoded successfully | User document retrieved from database using ObjectId | Authentication succeeds | Protected API endpoints return 200 OK | Frontend protected routes render correctly

Title: Password Hashing and Security
Preconditions: New user registration occurs | Password is provided in plain text
Steps: 1. Register user with password | 2. Verify password is hashed before storage | 3. Verify bcrypt is used for hashing | 4. Attempt login with correct password | 5. Verify password verification works
Test Data: Password: SecurePass@123
Expected Result: Password is hashed using bcrypt | Plain text password is NEVER stored | Hash is stored in MongoDB users.password field | Login succeeds with correct password | Password verification uses bcrypt.verify() | NFR-001 security requirement satisfied

Title: Real-Time Spot Availability Update
Preconditions: Multiple spots exist | Some are available, some are occupied | User is on /reservations page
Steps: 1. Load parking spots via GET /api/spots | 2. Verify is_available flag for each spot | 3. Create a reservation for spot B10 | 4. Reload spots | 5. Verify spot B10 is now marked unavailable
Test Data: Initial: 20 available spots | After reservation: 19 available spots | Spot B10: is_available false
Expected Result: Spots display in real-time with correct availability | Available spots show green border | Occupied spots show gray background and "Occupied" status | Spot availability updates after reservation | Available count decreases | Occupied spots are non-clickable

Title: Audit Logging for Admin Actions
Preconditions: Admin user is authenticated | Admin performs various management actions | MongoDB audit_logs collection exists
Steps: 1. Admin views metrics | 2. Admin views users list | 3. Admin cancels a reservation | 4. Admin deletes a user | 5. Query audit_logs collection in MongoDB
Test Data: Admin user_id | Various admin actions
Expected Result: Each admin action creates an audit log entry | Log includes: user_id, action (view/cancel/delete), resource (metrics/users/reservations), timestamp | Details field captures relevant IDs | Logs are immutable | NFR-002 audit requirement satisfied

Title: Login with Invalid Credentials
Preconditions: User account exists in database | User attempts login with wrong password
Steps: 1. Navigate to /login | 2. Enter valid email but incorrect password | 3. Click Login button
Test Data: Email: admin@oktawave.com | Password: WrongPassword123
Expected Result: HTTP 401 Unauthorized returned | Error message: "Invalid email or password" | No JWT token issued | User remains on login page | No session created | Password hash comparison fails

Title: Login with Non-Existent User
Preconditions: No user exists with given email in database
Steps: 1. Navigate to /login | 2. Enter non-existent email and any password | 3. Submit login form
Test Data: Email: nonexistent@test.com | Password: AnyPassword123
Expected Result: HTTP 401 Unauthorized returned | Generic error message: "Invalid email or password" (no user enumeration) | No JWT token issued | User remains on login page | No database query succeeds

Title: Access Protected Route Without Authentication
Preconditions: User is NOT authenticated | No JWT token in localStorage | User attempts to access protected route
Steps: 1. Clear localStorage | 2. Navigate directly to /dashboard URL | 3. Verify redirect behavior
Test Data: No authentication credentials
Expected Result: User is redirected to /login | Protected content is not rendered | PrivateRoute component blocks access | useAuth hook returns null token | All protected routes (/dashboard, /vehicles, /reservations, /admin) are inaccessible

Title: Expired JWT Token Handling
Preconditions: User has expired JWT token in localStorage | Token exp claim is in the past
Steps: 1. Set expired token in localStorage | 2. Attempt to make authenticated API call | 3. Observe error handling
Test Data: Expired JWT token (exp < current UTC time)
Expected Result: Backend JWT decode fails | HTTP 401 Unauthorized returned | Error: "Could not validate credentials" | Frontend receives 401 | User is logged out | localStorage is cleared | User redirected to /login

Title: Admin Delete Admin User
Preconditions: Admin user is authenticated | Admin attempts to delete another admin user | Target user has role = 'admin'
Steps: 1. Login as admin | 2. Navigate to Users tab | 3. Attempt to delete a user with admin role | 4. Click Delete button
Test Data: Target user: another admin user
Expected Result: HTTP 400 Bad Request returned | Error message: "Cannot delete admin users" | Admin user is NOT deleted from database | Safety check in backend prevents admin deletion | Audit log may still record the attempt
